reason: The statement that Yahweh is described as a vengeful God whose violence hits many people and not just a handful applies to the text fragment because it includes instances of divine punishment and violence, such as the LORD departing from Saul and bringing calamity upon him and his sons, as well as the mention of executing fierce wrath upon Amalek. These actions demonstrate a portrayal of Yahweh as a deity capable of inflicting harm and punishment on a larger scale beyond just a few individuals.
